root_text.sn22.127:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn22.127:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.127:2.1: “Idhāvuso assutavā puthujjano samudayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ ‘samudayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti; vayadhammaṁ rūpaṁ …pe… ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ rūpan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.127:2.2: Samudayadhammaṁ vedanaṁ …pe… vayadhammaṁ vedanaṁ …pe… ‘samudayavayadhammā vedanā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.127:2.4: samudayadhamme saṅkhāre …pe… vayadhamme saṅkhāre …pe… samudayavayadhamme saṅkhāre ‘samudayavayadhammā saṅkhārā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.127:2.5: Samudayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ …pe… samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘samudayavayadhammaṁ viññāṇan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.

translation_text.sn22.127:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana. …
translation_text.sn22.127:1.3: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.
translation_text.sn22.127:1.5: And how is an ignorant person defined?”
translation_text.sn22.127:2.1: “Reverend, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t truly understand form, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as form which is liable to originate and fall.
translation_text.sn22.127:2.5: consciousness, which is liable to originate … liable to fall … liable to originate and fall, as consciousness which is liable to originate and fall.
translation_text.sn22.127:2.7: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”
